cent: noun. A subunit of currency equal to one-hundredth of the main unit of currency in many countries.
center: noun. The point in the interior of a circle that is equidistant from all points on the circumference.

centre: noun. Australia, UK, Canada, Ireland, New Zealand, and South Africa standard spelling of center.

cero: noun. A large scombroid food fish (Scomberomorus regalis) found chiefly in the West Indies.
clone: noun. A living organism (originally a plant) produced asexually from a single ancestor, to which it is genetically identical.
clot: noun. A thrombus, solidified mass of blood.
col: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Columbia-Wenatchi.
con: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cofán.
cone: noun. A surface of revolution formed by rotating a segment of a line around another line that intersects the first line.
cor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Cornish.
core: noun. In general usage, an essential part of a thing surrounded by other essential things.
corn: noun. Any cereal plant (or its grain) that is the main crop or staple of a country or region.
cornel: noun. Any tree or shrub of the dogwood subgenera, Cornus subg.
cornet: noun. A musical instrument of the brass family, slightly smaller than a trumpet, usually in the musical key of B-flat.
creel: noun. A woven basket, especially a wicker basket.
crenel: noun. The space between merlons in a crenelated battlement.
creole: noun. A language formed from two or more languages which has developed from a pidgin to become a first language.
eel: noun. Any freshwater fish of the order Anguilliformes, which are elongated and resemble snakes.

enol: noun. An organic compound containing a hydroxyl group bonded to a carbon atom which is doubly bonded to another carbon atom.
enrol: verb. UK, New Zealand, Australia, Ireland, and Canada standard spelling of enroll.
enter: verb. To go or come into an enclosed or partially enclosed space.
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
erect: adjective. Upright; vertical or reaching broadly upwards.
erne: noun. A sea eagle (Haliaeetus), especially the white-tailed eagle (Haliaeetus albicilla)
lectern: noun. A stand with a slanted top used to support a Bible from which passages are read during a church service.
lector: noun. A lay person who reads aloud certain religious texts in a church service.

lore: noun. All the facts and traditions about a particular subject that have been accumulated over time through education or experience.

nee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kumak.

one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.

tee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Huehuetla Tepehua.
teen: noun. Synonym of teenager: a person between 13 and 19 years old (inclusive).

ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
tenor: noun. A musical range or section higher than bass and lower than alto.
tenrec: noun. Any of several diverse small mammals, of the family Tenrecidae, many native to Madagascar.
toe: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Tomedes.
tole: noun. A decorative metalware having a lacquered or enamelled surface that is painted or gilded.
ton: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tongan.
toner: noun. Powder used in laser printers and photocopiers to form the text and images on the printed paper.
tor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Togbo-Vara Banda.
